my_thoughts: The rejection works: not single component, holistic (more centres/parts = better), logical method = mechanical part only. The student attempted reduction (attempting to reduce method), which shows they understand: psychological method = intellectual part (single component). They'll likely ask about esoteric method next.
accumulated_insight: T2: Student attempted reduction ('Is the psychological method the use of the intellectual part of the intellectual centre?')—attempting to reduce method via proposes simplification and seeks single component. They understand: psychological method = intellectual part (single component). The comparison_contrast mechanism rejects: not single component, holistic (more centres/parts = better), logical method = mechanical part only ('No, not quite. The more centres and the more parts of centres you use, the better the psychological method. It means using all the powers you have. Logical method means the use of only the mechanical part of intellectual centre'). Pattern: method question → validation → reduction attempt → rejection → [next: esoteric question]. They're grasping: psychological method = holistic (multiple centres), logical = mechanical part only (rejection).
visible_response:
No, not quite. The more centres and the more parts of centres you use, the better the
psychological method.
It means using all the powers you have. Logical method means
the use of only the mechanical part of intellectual centre.
